A Journey Planned Just For You

A Rhine River Cruise for John & Carol

Looked after from your front door and back · 12–19 August 2026 · Basel to Amsterdam

Everything below is arranged around an easy, unhurried pace — the ship, the places you’ll see, and exactly how you get there and home again.

This trip is built around one simple promise: you’re looked after from your own front door in Newport all the way to the ship, and all the way home again — with the drivers and porters handling your cases, and a wheelchair for you at every airport. Below is the cruise, the ship, the places you’ll see, the day-by-day, and exactly what it all involves.

An easy, unhurried holiday.
  • No heavy lifting. The drivers and the ship’s porters handle your cases, and staff help you through the airports — so you’re not dragging bags around.
  • A wheelchair whenever it helps. At each airport, an assistant meets you with a wheelchair and takes you straight through — all arranged for you.
  • Go at your own pace. A lift on board, and relaxed excursion choices every day — do as much or as little as you fancy, with a wine tasting or two along the way and always a comfortable spot to sit and watch the river go by.

Your Cruise

Scenic “A taste of the Rhine – A wine cruise” — seven nights sailing from Basel to Amsterdam aboard the intimate, all-suite Scenic Pearl, departing Wednesday 12 August 2026, with vineyard visits and wine tastings woven through the sightseeing.

It’s a small, calm, beautifully-run ship — all-suite, with a lift, and everything genuinely all-inclusive — with gentle, optional excursions so you can do as much or as little as you like each day. You’re looked after every step of the way, with the flights and every transfer arranged for you.

A gentle start. You fly direct from Bristol to Basel and have two easy, tram-based days in Basel before boarding — time to settle in and enjoy the city at your own pace, no rushing. And an August sailing keeps September free for Brian’s 80th in Jesolo.

The Ship & Your Suite

Scenic Pearl is a small, all-suite ship — calm, beautifully finished, with a lift between all the decks. Every room is a suite with a butler; yours is a Balcony Suite with Scenic’s signature full-width “Sun Lounge” window that lowers at the touch of a button, so your room opens right onto the passing river.

It’s on the middle deck, where the restaurant is too — so your suite, the dining room and the lounge are all just a step or a short lift-ride apart, with barely any walking.

Ship & suite photographs © Scenic.

Your Journey — Door to Door

Every transfer is assisted and every bag is carried for you. A wheelchair is on hand at each airport through “special assistance” (arranged for you), and the driver stays with you until airport staff take over — so there’s never a moment alone with the luggage.

  1. Collected at your front door in Newport by a private car, and driven to Bristol Airport (~45 min), dropping level and step-free right by the terminal. The driver handles the bags and walks you to the assistance point.
  2. easyJet flight, Bristol → Basel (direct, ~1h40). A pre-booked wheelchair takes you and your bags through a dedicated assistance lane to the gate and onto the plane — no steps, no queues.
  3. Met off the plane at Basel and taken to arrivals with your bags; a private car is waiting for the short ride into the city.
  4. Two easy days at a comfortable, central Basel hotel (air-conditioned, with a lift) (see Your Two Days in Basel below), then a private car to the ship.
  5. Aboard Scenic Pearl in Basel. Porters take your cases straight to your suite; you’re helped aboard with crew on hand.
  6. Seven nights down the Rhine — Strasbourg & Alsace, Baden-Baden, Heidelberg & the vineyards, Koblenz and the castle-lined Rhine Gorge, Cologne — then Amsterdam (day-by-day below).
  7. In Amsterdam, a short private car takes you from the ship to the airport, then a KLM flight home to Cardiff (~1h20), with airport assistance again — and a private car to your Newport door (~25 min).

The Whole Trip, Day by Day

Your whole journey at a glance, with times where we have them. Nothing to plan — just follow along.

DateWhereYour day
Mon 10 AugHome → BaselCar to Bristol (~45 min); flight 18:20 → 21:00; private car to your Basel hotel; supper nearby.
Tue 11 AugBaselA free day — the Beyeler museum (tram 6), a 75-min river cruise, and dinner at Restaurant Kunsthalle.
Wed 12 AugBasel → aboardMorning by the Rhine; ~11:30am private car to the ship; board from noon; welcome reception.
Thu 13 AugStrasbourg & AlsaceCathedral & Petite France, or an Alsace wine-village tasting.
Fri 14 AugBaden-BadenElegant spa town or the thermal baths; evening palace concert with sparkling wine.
Sat 15 AugHeidelberg & the PfalzCastle & old town, or a Riesling vineyard tour & tasting.
Sun 16 AugKoblenz & the Rhine GorgeKoblenz old town; then the castle-lined gorge from the sun deck, wine in hand.
Mon 17 AugCologneOld town & cathedral, a short flat walk from the ship.
Tue 18 AugAmsterdamArrive; a canal cruise or the Fabrique des Lumières light show.
Wed 19 AugAmsterdam → homeStay aboard for breakfast & lunch (your cabin’s vacated in the morning); Scenic’s transfer to the airport; flight 16:25 → 16:45 to Cardiff; private car home.

The Route

Seven nights down the Rhine, Basel to Amsterdam — through the Rhineland and the Netherlands, with the castle-lined UNESCO gorge at its heart. Numbers mark the day of the cruise.

The Places You’ll See

Destination photos via Wikimedia Commons: Amsterdam © Basile Morin; Cologne © Ahgee; Marksburg/Rhine — Gary Todd (CC0); Heidelberg © Mikheil Baramidze; Baden-Baden © Franzfoto; Strasbourg © Dietmar Rabich; Basel © Matti Blume. Licensed CC BY-SA / CC0.

On the Rhine — Day by Day

Seven nights, Basel to Amsterdam. Each port offers a choice of included excursions (Scenic’s “Freechoice”), so you pick what suits you — plus a special “Enrich” evening (a private classical concert in a baroque palace) and, on this sailing, wine tastings and a vineyard visit woven through. The last column simply shows the pace of each day.

Easy gentle, off the ship   Coach a bus ride to the sight   Gentle pace a bit more walking — take your time

DayPort & sailingIncluded excursions (effort)Walk or coach?The pace
1
Wed		 Basel (Switzerland) — embark afternoon; overnight in port Board & settle in; welcome reception aboard (Relaxed) Dockside Easy Settle in aboard, rested after the Basel stay.
2
Thu		 Kehl (for Strasbourg, France) & Alsace — sail on in the evening Strasbourg walking tour — Cathedral, Petite France (Active); Alsace wine village + cellar tasting (Moderate, wine); e-bike city tour (Active) Coach short ~15 min across the Rhine into Strasbourg Gentle pace Strasbourg is flat but cobbled and a fair walk; the Alsace wine-village + cellar tasting is the gentler, more seated choice.
3
Fri		 Rastatt / Plittersdorf (for Baden-Baden) — day ashore Baden-Baden spa-town walk (Relaxed); Caracalla thermal baths (Relaxed); Schloss Favorite palace (Moderate). Evening Scenic Enrich: private concert, Rastatt Baroque Palace, with a sparkling-wine reception (Relaxed, seated) Coach Baden-Baden is ~20 km inland Easy A gentle coach day — Baden-Baden is flat and elegant; the thermal-spa option is very low-effort, and the evening palace concert is all seated.
4
Sat		 Mannheim (for Heidelberg & the Pfalz vineyards) — day ashore Heidelberg orientation walk (Moderate); Heidelberg + Castle (Moderate); Fritz Ritter vineyard tour & Riesling tasting in the Pfalz (Moderate, wine) Coach Heidelberg ~20 km / 30–40 min; the vineyard a similar coach ride Gentle pace Heidelberg has cobbles and an uphill castle — take the gentle orientation tour, or the town at your own pace. The vineyard tour & tasting is the more relaxed, mostly-seated alternative.
5
Sun		 Koblenz + Rhine Gorge scenic cruising — sail the UNESCO gorge of castles Koblenz old-town walk (Moderate); Koblenz Wine Museum (Relaxed); Rhine Gorge cruise with a regional-wine tasting & talk on deck (Relaxed, seated) Koblenz walkable (flat riverside); the gorge is enjoyed aboard Easy The loveliest afternoon of the week — seated on the sun deck as the castles pass, glass of Riesling in hand.
6
Mon		 Cologne (Köln) — arrive; evening free; sail overnight to Amsterdam Old Town + Cathedral walking tour (Moderate); Farina fragrance museum (Relaxed) Walkable — ship moors by the old town, ~5–10 min flat to the cathedral Easy A gentle dockside day; old-town squares are cobbled (skip the tower climb).
7
Tue		 Amsterdam — arrive; overnight in port Canal cruise (Relaxed); Fabrique des Lumières digital-art show (Relaxed); old-city walking tour (Moderate) Dockside / very short transfer Easy The canal cruise or light show are seated and a lovely way to finish.
8
Wed		 Amsterdam — disembark after breakfast; direct flight home from Amsterdam — (disembarkation) Transfer to Amsterdam (Schiphol) airport Easy Relaxed departure — the direct flight home runs most days.

In short: the Rhine-Gorge afternoon (Day 5), Cologne (Day 6), Amsterdam (Day 7) and the Baden-Baden spa day (Day 3) are the easy, relaxed ones. Strasbourg (Day 2) and Heidelberg (Day 4) involve a bit more walking — and both have a gentler, mostly-seated wine-themed alternative you can take instead. Nothing is ever compulsory.

Note: This is Scenic’s wine-themed “A taste of the Rhine” sailing (Basel→Amsterdam) — the same ports as the standard Rhine Highlights, with wine tastings and a vineyard visit added. Exact arrival, departure and sailing times are confirmed in the final Scenic documents; excursion menus can vary slightly by departure.

Your Two Days in Basel

You fly out on the Monday and have two relaxed days in Basel before boarding the ship on the Wednesday — a gentle, unhurried start, on a direct flight with no changing planes. At check-in you’re given a free BaselCard (every tram free), and a private car is booked for you for the airport and the ship. (Don’t worry about the language — Basel is German-speaking, but everyone speaks English.)

Your hotel — one of two lovely central spots. Depending on the weather you’ll be in one of two comfortable central hotels — either Hotel Krafft, right on the Rhine, or the Radisson Blu, which is air-conditioned for a warm week. It’s confirmed about a week before you travel — and either way your days are exactly the same, because both are on tram 6, so the museum, the boat and dinner are all one easy tram, no changes.

Monday — arriving

  • ~9:00pm — You land at Basel. An assistant meets you at the plane with a wheelchair and brings you to arrivals, where your driver is waiting — about 15 minutes in a private car to your hotel.
  • Check in, collect your BaselCard (free trams), and have a light supper nearby. Then rest.

Tuesday — your day (unhurried, with time to rest)

A gentle rhythm: one outing in the morning, lunch, a rest, then something easy. Do as much or as little as you like — none of it is fixed.

  • 9:30am — breakfast at the hotel.
  • 10:45am — the Fondation Beyeler (Basel’s loveliest art museum). From your hotel’s tram stop, take Tram 6 towards “Riehen, Grenze” to the “Fondation Beyeler” stop — the museum is right there, all on one level, with chairs throughout.
  • ~12:30pm — lunch in the museum’s garden restaurant, then Tram 6 back to your stop. Have a rest.
  • 3:00pm — the river boat (optional). The 75-minute sightseeing cruise from the Schifflände jetty — a lift at the pier and on the boat, so step-free, and you’re seated the whole way. Don’t fancy it? Just sit by the Rhine.
  • 7:30pm — dinner at a lovely spot a few minutes from your hotel.

Whichever hotel: from Krafft your tram stop is Rheingasse (right at the door) and dinner is KLARA, a few minutes away; from the Radisson your stop is Heuwaage (a minute away) and dinner is Restaurant Kunsthalle, a short walk. Both put you one tram from everything.

Wednesday — boarding day

  • Morning — a leisurely breakfast and a last look at Basel. Nothing to rush.
  • ~11:30am — your private car takes you the short ride to the ship (boarding from noon), and your cruise begins.

Who Sorts What

Almost everything is either included in the cruise or arranged for you in advance. Just three small things are yours to do.

Included in your cruise

  • ✓ Your 7-night cruise in a Balcony Suite
  • ✓ Butler; all meals aboard
  • ✓ All drinks, all day (incl. the wine tastings)
  • ✓ Every excursion — sightseeing & wine
  • ✓ All tips & gratuities
  • ✓ Palace concert; Wi-Fi; laundry
  • ✓ Porters take your cases to your cabin

Arranged for you

  • ✓ The cruise & your Balcony Suite
  • ✓ Your flights (direct) — Bristol out, Cardiff home
  • ✓ Your airport wheelchair & assistance
  • ✓ Your Basel hotel & your two days in Basel
  • ✓ Private cars, door to airport and back
  • ✓ Your private car at the airport and the ship
  • ✓ Your dinner reservations
  • ✓ Your phone set up before you go

Just for you to do

  • • Check your travel insurance covers the trip & bring the details
  • • Passports in date; pack your medicines in your hand luggage
  • • Be ready at the front door — the car does the rest

A word on the bags: the drivers load and unload them, the airport staff help you through, and the ship’s porters take them to your cabin — so you’re never dragging cases around (you just hand them over at the airline check-in as usual).

What It Costs

Every car is a private Mercedes V-Class. From your front door in Newport, to Basel, to the airport in Amsterdam — every transfer is a roomy Mercedes van: easy to step into, plenty of space for both cases, and a driver to load and unload them. The car prices below are now firm quotes, not estimates.
ItemCost
Scenic cruise — Balcony Suite 204, 12 Aug (all-inclusive onboard + Scenic-arranged direct flights; live sale price)£5,790
Flights, 2 pax — easyJet Bristol→Basel + KLM Amsterdam→Cardiff (direct, both confirmed; Scenic-arranged & included — 23kg hold + cabin bags, speedy boarding, seats)included
Private Mercedes V-Class transfers (Newport→Bristol; Basel airport→hotel & hotel→ship; Cardiff→Newport — Amsterdam→airport by Scenic)£457
Basel river-boat cruise (the two of you)£30
Hotel in central Basel, 2 nights (incl. breakfast; air-conditioned, lift)£600
Meals in Basel — supper, lunch & a special dinner, coffees (breakfasts included at the hotel; Wed lunch aboard the ship)£300
Airport wheelchair & special assistance (arranged by Scenic)Free
Travel insurance (already covered by your worldwide annual policy)Covered
Total, the two of you — door to door≈ £7,200

The cruise price is Scenic’s live sale price for your suite; the car and hotel figures are firm quotes we’ll pin down exactly when booking. There’s plenty of room to upgrade the suite or add a night if you’d like.

The Last Few Details

Everything is researched and ready; when you’re happy to go ahead, these are the only things left to sort:

  • Your flights, cars, hotel and dinners are all being arranged for you.
  • Your travel insurance is already sorted — your worldwide annual policy covers it.

Good to Know

  • Both flights are direct and from close to home — Bristol out (~45 min away), and home into Cardiff (only ~25 min from your door on the tired leg). No connections to catch.
  • Bristol is easy with assistance. The car drops you step-free right by the terminal, and airport staff meet you there with a wheelchair and take you — and the bags — all the way to the plane. No steps, no queues.
  • On board, no stairs to manage. The lift reaches your suite, the restaurant and reception — the restaurant is even on your own deck. (Only the open-air sun deck up top is by stairs, as on every river ship.)
  • Everything onboard is included — all meals, all drinks, every excursion and all the wine tastings, tips and the evening concert. Once you’re aboard, there’s nothing more to pay and nothing to organise.

The next step is simply to say the word, and we confirm your suite and the flights and book it. You’ll then get a final day-by-day to keep, with every time and detail confirmed. No rush and no obligation — have a look and see what you think.

Put together with care, just for the two of you. Nothing is booked yet — this is simply to show you the trip.
